macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Surges Past $90 as US-Iran Conflict Escalates in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Brent crude jumped 3% to surpass $90 a barrel amid expanded US and Iran attacks curbing energy shipments, raising inflation fears and pushing odds of a July Fed rate hike higher.

items.title: Fed Rate-Hike Voices Swell Ahead of July Decision; Warsh Faces Dissent Risk
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined policymakers arguing for rate hikes to combat persistent inflation, setting up a charged debate at Chairman Kevin Warsh's second meeting with potential dissents.

items.title: US Stocks Fall as Chip Selloff Deepens and AI Trade Faces Earnings Test
items.summary: A rotation out of recent rally winners sent chip stocks toward their steepest weekly decline in over a year, while investors await Alphabet and Intel earnings to validate the AI surge amid Gulf conflict uncertainty.

items.title: Apple Regains World's Most Valuable Company Spot, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple reclaimed the top spot with a $4.88 trillion valuation after Nvidia dropped 3.5%, marking a shift in the AI-driven market leadership.

items.title: Morgan Stanley Posts Record Quarterly Revenue as Equities Trading Surges 69%
items.summary: Morgan Stanley reported record Q2 revenue and profit driven by a 69% surge in equities trading, reflecting Wall Street's 'sweet spot' fueled by SpaceX IPO and Iran war volatility.

items.title: China Holds Benchmark Lending Rates Unchanged for 14th Month Despite Soft Q2 Data
items.summary: China is set to keep lending rates unchanged in July despite Q2 economic growth slowing to 4.3%, the weakest pace since 2022, highlighting uneven growth in the world's second-largest economy.

items.title: Gold Slips Below $4,000 as Oil Advance and Fed Hike Fears Weigh on Prices
items.summary: Gold prices declined as oil surged and growing Fed rate-hike voices increased inflation concerns, with the S&P GSCI Crude Oil Index rising 2.45% in the past 24 hours.

items.title: US Labor Market Weakness Hidden: 105.8 Million Americans Outside Labor Force
items.summary: FRED data shows the number of Americans outside the labor force reached an all-time high of 105.8 million in June, suggesting the US labor market is weaker than headline unemployment rates indicate.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $64K as U.S.-Iran Tensions Squeeze Crypto Markets
items.summary: Bitcoin hovered between $64,000 and $65,000 amid the ninth consecutive night of U.S. airstrikes on Iran, while Ethereum outperformed with a +0.78% gain to ~$1,860 and Solana rose 1.40%.

items.title: Citadel Securities Invests $400M in Crypto.com at $20B Valuation
items.summary: Citadel Securities made a major institutional move by investing $400 million in Crypto.com, valuing the exchange at $20 billion, signaling growing TradFi confidence in crypto infrastructure.

items.title: U.S. Agencies Miss GENIUS Act Deadline for Final Stablecoin Rules
items.summary: U.S. regulatory agencies failed to meet the 2026 deadline for finalizing stablecoin regulations under the GENIUS Act, creating uncertainty as the USDT market continues to boom.

items.title: Cardano Activates Van Rossem Hard Fork, Upgrading to Protocol Version 11
items.summary: Cardano successfully activated its Van Rossem hard fork on July 18, upgrading to Protocol Version 11 to deliver faster, cheaper smart contracts and enhanced node security.

items.title: South Korea Sanctions Dunamu (Upbit Parent) Over $30M Solana Hack
items.summary: South Korea's Financial Supervisory Service initiated sanctions against Dunamu, the parent of Upbit, following a $30 million hack of Upbit's Solana hot wallet, setting a potential regulatory precedent.

items.title: BlackRock Buys $350M in Digital Assets Over Five Days
items.summary: BlackRock expanded its cryptocurrency exposure by purchasing approximately $350 million worth of digital assets over a five-day period, reinforcing institutional adoption trends.

items.title: Tether USDT Faces Two-Year Compliance Countdown with 25% Reserve Risk
items.summary: Tether's USDT faces a two-year compliance deadline, with analysts warning that roughly 25% of its reserves may not meet upcoming regulatory standards.

items.title: Filecoin Drops 3.5% Amid Technical Selling Pressure
items.summary: Filecoin (FIL) fell 3.5% over the past 24 hours due to technical selling, while broader market sentiment remained pessimistic amid stablecoin outflows exceeding $2.3 billion from Binance and Bybit.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Launches o4 Reasoning Model with 5M Token Context Window
items.summary: OpenAI released the o4 reasoning model on July 18, 2026, featuring a 5 million token context window and achieving 94% on the GPQA benchmark, setting a new standard for complex multi-step problem solving in enterprise applications.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion-parameter model claimed to be the world's largest open-weight AI system, delivering performance approaching Anthropic's frontier Fable model and challenging top U.S. systems.

items.title: Anthropic Deploys Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Fable and Mythos Models
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 and restored access to its Fable and Mythos frontier models following a federal export control review, marking a significant update to its model lineup.

items.title: Google DeepMind Releases GenCeption for Unified Computer Vision Breakthroughs
items.summary: Google DeepMind launched GenCeption, a video generation-based model that unifies depth estimation, image segmentation, and 3D pose estimation, challenging the dominance of specialized models like Segment Anything.

items.title: Perplexity AI Secures $1.1B Series D at $14B Valuation
items.summary: Perplexity AI closed a $1.1 billion Series D funding round on July 17, 2026, reaching a $14 billion post-money valuation, reflecting strong investor confidence in AI search and reasoning capabilities.

items.title: Apple Files Lawsuit Against OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft
items.summary: Apple accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets on Friday as it pursues building its own hardware for ChatGPT, escalating tensions between the tech giant and the AI company.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Data Center Moratorium to Protect Energy Grid
items.summary: New York will block new large AI data centers for up to a year, marking the country's first statewide moratorium aimed at protecting the environment and energy grid from AI infrastructure expansion.

items.title: Stanford Team Achieves 92% on SWE-Bench with Open SWE-Agent
items.summary: Stanford researchers released the open SWE-Agent, scoring 92% on the SWE-Bench software engineering benchmark using a 70B parameter model, surpassing all prior closed systems.

items.title: Google Makes Veo 3 AI Video Creation Tool Widely Available
items.summary: Google has made its most powerful AI video creator, Veo 3, available for everyone on its Vertex AI platform, expanding access to advanced video generation capabilities.

items.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron3Embed as Top Open-Source Embedding Model
items.summary: NVIDIA introduced the Nemotron3Embed series, with its 8B version ranking first on the RTEB benchmark, becoming the strongest open-source embedding model for production RAG and agent retrieval.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Capcom Restores Onimusha: Way of the Sword Pre-Orders for Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: Capcom officially announced the restoration of digital pre-orders for Onimusha: Way of the Sword on Nintendo Switch 2 after a brief suspension, confirming the game's continued development for the platform.

items.title: MAGES. Confirms Steins;Gate RE:BOOT Release Date for August 2026
items.summary: MAGES. announced that the fully remastered Steins;Gate RE:BOOT will launch on August 20, 2026, across Nintendo Switch 2, Nintendo Switch, and PS5.

items.title: Call of Duty League Championship 2026: Riyadh Falcons Surprise LA Thieves
items.summary: At the Call of Duty League Championship Weekend 2026 in Riyadh, the Riyadh Falcons defeated the Los Angeles Thieves in a surprising upset match.

items.title: GTA VI Pre-Orders Hit $260M in First Week, Potential $5B Launch
items.summary: Reports indicate Grand Theft Auto VI generated $260 million in pre-orders during its first week, with analysts projecting a potential $5 billion launch revenue.

items.title: Netflix Confirms 300 Titles Used Generative AI
items.summary: Netflix officially confirmed that approximately 300 of its content titles have utilized generative AI technology in their production.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Becomes World's Most Valuable Company, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple overtook Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, reshuffling tech heavyweight rankings as investors reassess the artificial intelligence outlook.

items.title: SpaceX Targets July 23 for Next Starship Rocket Launch Attempt
items.summary: SpaceX announced on Sunday it is targeting Thursday, July 23, for another attempt to launch its Starship rocket after previous setbacks.

items.title: TSMC Invests Additional $100 Billion to Expand Arizona AI Chip Facilities
items.summary: TSMC expects strong, multi-year demand for AI chips and is investing a further $100 billion to expand its Arizona manufacturing facilities, though it faces construction worker shortages.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Moratorium on New Large Data Centers
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers that fuel artificial intelligence for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, marking the country's first statewide moratorium.

items.title: EU Charging Infrastructure Outpaces Electric Vehicle Sales Across Member States
items.summary: Charging infrastructure in the European Union has outpaced EV sales, with all member states except Malta meeting public charging requirements linked to their EV fleet sizes.

items.title: Netflix Acquires Ben Affleck's AI Filmmaking Startup InterPositive for $587 Million
items.summary: Netflix revealed it paid $587 million in cash for InterPositive, a startup co-founded by Ben Affleck focused on AI filmmaking technology.

items.title: Zoox Issues Software Recall After Robotaxi Confused by Heavy Smoke
items.summary: Zoox issued a software recall after a robotaxi got confused by heavy smoke, following warnings from the U.S. automotive safety regulator about AV vehicles interfering with first responders.

items.title: Amazon Leo Satellite Internet Service Set to Launch in South Africa in 2027
items.summary: Amazon announced it will launch its new satellite internet service, Amazon Leo, in South Africa in 2027.

items.title: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Launches First Constellation of Space-Computing Project
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co. launched the first constellation of a space-computing project that aims to deploy 1,000 satellites in space.

items.title: Abbott Laboratories Investigates Two Cyber Incidents at Cancer Diagnostics Business
items.summary: Abbott Laboratories is investigating two cyber incidents involving unauthorized access to internal systems at its cancer diagnostics business and LabCentral portal, though operations were not affected.
